threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look bewildered ordering a flat white at a café, like it’s a complex science experiment rather than just coffee.
Caught the 59 last Saturday; a bloke in full suit argued with the driver about which stop was his ‘unfairly abandoned’ home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a cat chilling on top of a food truck in Hermannplatz while the owner was off chatting with a friend.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you see people lining up at 3 AM for a kebab like it’s a cultural rite. It’s part of the nightlife ritual here.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they instinctively know to avoid Civic Center BART when a dog’s on the tracks—always a good excuse to skip that commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy feeding pigeons on the tracks at Civic Center yesterday. Muni crew just shrugged and let the chaos unfold. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just one big festival. In reality, the locals are often bemoaning the noise and wishing for a quiet evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their confusion over the 'siesta' timing—still trying to get things done during lunch but missing out on the real rhythm of the day.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You think you can just hop on the Tube anytime? Good luck in the mornings—it's a game of survival and scheduling your life around strikes.
Caught the 59 last night; two blokes pretending to be on a game show, shouting trivia at each other. Bus driver just rolled his eyes but smiled.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the call to prayer from the mosque mixing with the bass from the open-air bar down the street. Such a Berlin moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar where the drinks are served by drag queens and the walls are covered in retro video game art. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their reaction to a dog on the tracks—most just nod, while the transplants panic like it's a state of emergency.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo casually hop on the bus with a top hat in hand—only in SF do you get a wedding right after a Wednesday morning meeting.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into a plaça and find a table; locals know that at 7 PM, it's all about fighting for a spot at the bar.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still marveling at the street performers on La Rambla, while the locals just walk by, unfazed.
